contentBlocks.original:
मूलतो ब्रह्मरूपाय मध्यतो विष्णुरूपिणे ।
अग्रतः शिवरूपाय वृक्षराजाय ते नमः ।।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mūlato brahmarūpāya madhyato viṣṇurūpiṇe .
agrataḥ śivarūpāya vṛkṣarājāya te namaḥ ..
contentBlocks.translation: To you, O Vriksharaja (King of trees) who takes on the form of Brahma at the roots, Vishnu in the middle and Shiva at the extremes, our salutations
contentBlocks.meanings: मूलतः (mūlataḥ) – at the roots; ब्रह्मरूपाय (brahmarūpāya) – to the form of Brahma (brahma:Brahma | rūpāya:to the form); मध्यतः (madhyataḥ) – in the middle; विष्णुरूपिणे (viṣṇurūpiṇe) – to the form of Vishnu (viṣṇu:Vishnu | rūpiṇe:to the form); अग्रतः (agrataḥ) – at the extremes; शिवरूपाय (śivarūpāya) – to the form of Shiva (śiva:Shiva | rūpāya:to the form); वृक्षराजाय (vṛkṣarājāya) – to the King of trees (vṛkṣa:tree | rājāya:to the King); ते (te) – to you; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations;
